title: T-020 — API: Technical lab-results read model + Quality write bridge
prompt: Implement Phase 1 Technical lab-results read model and optional Quality service bridge per §5.5 + §10.6 + V-TEC-44. `lab_results` are Quality-owned. Technical GET endpoints may read/filter Quality-owned results. Technical POST must not write lab_results directly; it may call an existing Quality permission/service bridge (or return 501/blocked with explicit QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING if no bridge exists). ATP pass/fail calculation belongs in the Quality bridge contract; Technical can display/read result_status and threshold metadata. RED tests/api/lab-results.test.ts prove direct Technical writes are blocked and Quality bridge writes/readback work when bridge is present.

pipeline_inputs.description: Technical read model for Quality-owned lab results; writes only through Quality bridge.
pipeline_inputs.details: Ownership red-line: lab_results are Quality-owned. Technical may consume/read and request/write only through a Quality permission/service bridge if present. Do not model Technical as canonical authoring owner. If bridge is missing, make POST unavailable with explicit blocker rather than silently creating Technical-owned rows.
out_of_scope: UI Lab Results Log (T-067 docs gap)
acceptance_criteria: Given GET ?test_type=allergen_elisa&result_status=fail, then Technical returns only matching Quality-owned read-model rows.
Given Technical POST is called and no Quality bridge exists, then request fails with explicit QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING/501 and no lab_results row is written by Technical.
Given Quality bridge exists and caller has required Quality permission, then Technical bridge call delegates write and readback shows the Quality-owned row.
Given test_type=atp_swab result_status/threshold fields are present, then Technical displays/returns the Quality-calculated status without recalculating ownership locally.
test_strategy: RED tests covering V-TEC-44 trigger semantics.
risk_red_lines: Do not call external lab APIs (Phase 2).
Do not write lab_results directly from Technical-owned service/table code.
Do not model lab_results as Technical-owned canonical authoring; treat as Quality-owned read model/bridge for Phase 1.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Quality-owned lab_results write bridge/permission service if available; Technical must degrade explicitly when missing.
